To make matters worse, the kitchen is run by a chef called David Blaine who shares nothing of his famous namesake's magic when it comes to cooking - and whose threatening behaviour regularly gets him into trouble

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Part two of two. The chef continues his quest to transform the fortunes of the Burger Kitchen in Los Angeles, where tensions were running high because owner Alan Saffron had spent his son's entire inheritance on the struggling restaurant. Alan gets a wake-up call from internet critics, who he believes are trying to shut him down

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.sub-title: I Do, Deep in the Heart of Austin
programme.desc: Ten singles from Austin, Texas are ready to marry complete strangers in the ultimate quest for love. The first two couples say I do, sparks fly for one pair, but first impressions falter when one newly-wed clashes with their in-laws and another faces a shocking twist with their new family

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil enjoys sharing the secrets of the estate agent's life with Luke and Manny's classmates on school career day - only for things to turn ugly when his rival Gil Thorpe turns up. Jay's dream of being a writer looks destined to fail as he struggles to complete his book, while Lily's first encounter with the Tooth Fairy proves lucrative when a drunken Cam accidentally slips a $100 bill under her pillow

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Mitch's ex, successful doctor Teddy, invites the entire family to a fundraising event at the local roller rink - and when Cam realises how popular the host is, his jealousy soon gets out of hand. Claire tries to keep her distance from Jay to avoid talk of a job offer, while Phil has a novel way of teaching Gloria to skate and Haley gives Alex tips on flirting

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The family is recruited and brainwashed by a religious cult - except for Marge, who resorts to desperate measures to snap the others out of it

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart and Lisa go on a school trip, but Otto accidentally drives the bus off a bridge and into the water, where it washes up on a deserted island - leaving the schoolchildren to fend for themselves. Meanwhile, Homer decides to set up his own internet business. With the guest voice of James Earl Jones

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge goes on the offensive and plans a protest against a combat sport that has captivated Springfield's male population. When promoter Chett Englebrecht hears of her campaign, he challenges her to a fight - and agrees to shut down his entire operation if she beats him

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Dracula, the Wolf Man, the Mummy and Frankenstein's monster gatecrash a Halloween party at 742 Evergreen Terrace. Bart and Lisa plot revenge on their teachers in a Hitchcock-inspired short story, but end up turning on each another

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: For the first time in his career, Jake has a growing number of cases he just can't seem to solve, and the other detectives worry his run of bad luck will rub off on them. Holt asks Amy to take charge of a community outreach programme for at-risk youths, and Boyle helps Jeffords with a tough personal assignment. Comedy, starring Andy Samberg and Andre Braugher

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake meets an attractive medical examiner at a crime scene, but annoys his colleagues when his attempts to get together with her cause a hold-up with an autopsy report. Meanwhile, Amy discovers Jeffords has some hidden talents when the police sketch artist goes off sick

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Christmas has arrived, providing a typically chaotic time for the Pritchett-Dunphys, as Jay takes Manny to chop down a tree, Mitchell rushes to finish his shopping and Cam and Lily get into the holiday spirit at a charity event. Meanwhile, Gloria has a visit from her mum. Directed by Breaking Bad star Bryan Cranston

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil tries to share his love of dancing with unenthusiastic son Luke and Claire struggles to hide her frustration while teaching an overly cautious Alex to drive. Mitch and Cam enter the cut-throat world of wedding venue booking

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Gloria settles a score with another mum during a school visit, Mitch sets out to prove he is more environmentally aware than his neighbour, Manny and Luke have a double date with twins, and Alex goes to see a therapist. Jesse Eisenberg and Jane Krakowski guest star

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Jay is troubled when best pal Shorty reveals that he and his wife are moving away. Phil and Claire take Haley out to discuss her future, while Mitchell and Cam take a break from all the wedding planning to enjoy a romantic meal - only to interrupt a couple's surprise engagement dinner. Chazz Palminteri and Jennifer Tilly guest star

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and Marge try to breathe new life into their relationship by canoodling in public places, while Bart and Lisa use Grampa's metal detector to hunt for hidden treasure

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Blood-curdling tales from America's favourite family in another Halloween special. The spooky stories feature an island inhabited by 'manimals', plus tales about an army of Homer clones and zombie cowboys

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: How I Spent My Strummer Vacation
programme.desc: Homer is asked to help the Rolling Stones with a forthcoming benefit gig, filling his head with dreams of rock 'n' roll stardom. Unfortunately, they only want him to stand in as a roadie. Featuring the voices of Mick Jagger, Keith Richards, Lenny Kravitz, Tom Petty and Elvis Costello

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Bart v Lisa v the Third Grade
programme.desc: Lisa is thrilled to learn she has been moved up a grade at school - until she discovers Bart's poor performance has caused him to be demoted to the same class. Tensions inevitably develop between the pair, but a crisis during a field trip teaches them the value of working together. With the guest voice of Tony Bennett

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: At the police station, Charlie is feeling the pressure as he's pushed for answers on what caused him to be distracted. At the Loft, sick of Jenson and his remarks, Warren gives him money to leave the village. Frankie apologises to Caleb admitting she does like him and plans on doing the right thing

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge takes a job at the power plant to help with the cost of repairing the family home, which is sliding off its foundations. With the voice of Tom Jones

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.sub-title: Don't Mess with My Texas Wedding
programme.desc: Four more singles prepare to meet their matches at the altar, but it's not the first time some of them have said 'I do'. The two couples who've already tied the knot spend their first night together

programme.title: Celebs Go Dating
programme.desc: A very hungover David is grilled at the agency about his passionate date with Anthony, and Gabby heads out on the most important date of her journey so far. Lucinda's choices involve magic and vikings, and James' past might be about to catch up with him

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on the final episode of Peaky Blinders, as well as Made in Chelsea, Gordon Ramsay's Future Food Stars, Holidaying with Jane McDonald, Scam Interceptors and the ITV News about the energy price hikes

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.sub-title: Best Naughty Bits: Breaking Nude Ground
programme.desc: A look back at the times that the unique dating show has broken new ground, featuring stories from across the spectrum of gender and sexuality. There is also a catch-up with Lauren Harries, celebrated transsexual woman and the first celebrity to appear in a pod, and Josh, who was on the third series, to find out if he is still a virgin

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Singletons include political activist Lettie, who is 23 and looking for her intellectual equal, but despite being clued-up on politics, she is clueless on love. Meanwhile, octogenarian Doreen is not ready to give up on love and wants a man to keep her on her toes. Her date is 86-year-old author James

programme.title: Celebs Go Dating
programme.desc: A very hungover David is grilled at the agency about his passionate date with Anthony, and Gabby heads out on the most important date of her journey so far. Lucinda's choices involve magic and vikings, and James' past might be about to catch up with him

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.sub-title: Don't Mess with My Texas Wedding
programme.desc: Four more singles prepare to meet their matches at the altar, but it's not the first time some of them have said 'I do'. The two couples who've already tied the knot spend their first night together

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: At Pop-Pop's old apartment, Adam discovers an item from the past and realises they have more in common than he thought

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly learns that the complicated world of adult relationships is filled with minefields when she opens her mind to dating. And Barry discovers a new passion

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay is in New Jersey to help Rose Leone, who spent two years in a coma and came out of hospital to learn her son Michael had run her once-thriving Italian restaurant into the ground. With a menu of more than 126 dishes, the quality of the food is suffering, and even the chefs refuse to eat their own creations

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits a bar in Pittsburgh that serves Polish pierogi, and a Utah restaurant that specialises in seven varieties of Mexican mole

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Cooking
programme.desc: The food writer demonstrates how spicy food works well with a cool accompaniment, preparing a chicken curry and tomato stew with yoghurt, as well as a new version of a traditional pudding. programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Cooking
programme.desc: The food writer turns his attention to texture, demonstrating how it can be used to make food stand out. Among other things he prepares a classic savoury pie with a puff pastry crust, and a re-imagining of his favourite childhood pudding - poached pears. He also visits a seafront smokery in Brighton, where he meets a couple who make a soft and crisp recipe

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond serves up packed lunches to cowboys, including ranch hand sandwiches filled with pulled pork and a side of sweet potato crisps

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond demonstrates how to prepare her own take on comforting home cooking

programme.title: Jamie's Meat-Free Meals
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver makes a simple sticky onion tart, as well as a creamy coconut pumpkin rice with a spicy butter bean stew. He learns traditional ways to stuff vegetables in the Middle East, then turbo-charges aubergines with Indian spices

programme.title: Jamie's Meat-Free Meals
programme.desc: The former Naked Chef himself Jamie Oliver demonstrates more vibrant, hearty and healthy vegetarian dishes that everyone can add to their kitchen. In this final instalment, Jamie creates a beautiful oozy risotto with roasted tomatoes, and on a visit to Jerusalem, he is inspired by gorgeous breakfasts and rustles up pancakes supercharged with spinach, cheese and chilli. He also visits a cookery class with a healthy twist, and produces a show-stopping summer veg pie packed with plenty of good stuff

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa - Back to Basics
programme.sub-title: Cook Like a Pro: Turn Up the Volume

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa - Back to Basics
programme.desc: Ina gives traditional Thanksgiving dinner dishes a modern twist, with Tuscan turkey roulade, chipotle smashed sweet potatoes and pumpkin flan

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Matt Lucas and Noel Fielding present biscuit week. The signature challenge involves a sophisticated combination of fruit, nut and chocolate, and the bakers must then produce two takes on a tropical technical. For the showstopper round, they are challenged to create an elaborate table setting made entirely from biscuits, before judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith decide who will be crowned Star Baker and who will be leaving the contest this week

programme.title: Mary Berry Cooks
programme.desc: The food writer and Great British Bake Off judge demonstrates recipes for an outdoor summer lunch, including salmon fillets with herb sauce, ham with home-made chutney, and fillet of beef with garlic and mustard cream. programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Cooking
programme.desc: The food writer cooks with ingredients that share the same season or landscape, preparing an autumn dish of baked mushrooms and chestnuts, a blackberry and fig pie and a Mediterranean-inspired recipe using baked peppers, tomatoes and basil. He also visits a farm on Dartmoor to explore the link between the land that animals have been raised on and the taste of their meat

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond is shopping and sharing recipes for ground beef, putting together her slow cooker bolognese, a favourite that is perfect over pasta or layered in her polenta lasagna

programme.title: Jamie's Quick & Easy Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver offers viewers the ultimate set of go-to recipes for quick and easy-to-remember cooking having developed a collection of tasty dishes that each use just five ingredients, producing stress-free meals perfect for busy days and for those people who don't have the time or energy to devote to complicated cooking. On the menu in the first edition are seared scallops with black pudding, mashed potatoes and peas, as well as egg and mango chutney flatbreads, rib-eye steak, and an almond pastry puff

programme.title: Jamie's Quick & Easy Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver demonstrates four more stress-free recipes that each use only five ingredients each, including sesame seared tuna, a messy meatball bun and chocolate rye cookies. programme.title: Island of Terror (1966)
programme.desc: A pathologist investigates a series of gruesome deaths on a small island. He discovers that an experiment carried out by a group of doctors trying to find a cure for cancer has created a race of mutant creatures that suck the bones from the bodies of their human prey. Horror, starring Peter Cushing and Edward Judd

programme.title: Star Trek
programme.desc: A strange object gliding through outer space threatens to destroy the Enterprise and Kirk and the crew appear powerless to stop it. William Shatner, George Takei and Nichelle Nichols star

programme.title: The Six Million Dollar Man
programme.desc: A nuclear warhead comes into the possession of a veteran Japanese pilot. John Fujioka guest stars

programme.title: T.J. Hooker
programme.desc: A murderer's girlfriend needs police protection while waiting to testify against him. American drama, starring William Shatner

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: A gang of baby kidnappers are stopped, before the rangers find themselves rescuing people trapped inside a collapsing building

programme.title: Mobsters (1991)
programme.desc: Notorious gangsters Bugsy Siegel and Lucky Luciano and their cronies make a daring bid to overthrow the dons of Twenties New York. Crime thriller, starring Christian Slater, Patrick Dempsey, Richard Grieco, Anthony Quinn, F Murray Abraham and Michael Gambon

programme.title: Outlaw (2007)
programme.desc: An Iraq war veteran returns to an increasingly lawless England and discovers thugs have taken over his neighbourhood and his wife is having an affair. Pushed to the edge, he assembles and trains disillusioned men to take matters into their own hands and deal with the criminals who have wronged them. Crime drama, starring Sean Bean, Danny Dyer, Rupert Friend and Bob Hoskins

programme.title: No Mercy (1986)
programme.desc: Two Chicago cops pose as hitmen for an undercover sting, but the operation goes horribly wrong and one of them ends up dead. The surviving detective, bristling with anger and swearing revenge, heads for New Orleans in search of the kingpin behind the killing, but his only lead is the target's girlfriend. Action thriller, starring Richard Gere, Kim Basinger, George Dzundza and Jeroen Krabbe

programme.title: Rogue Hostage (2021)
programme.desc: Single father and former Marine Kyle Snowden, on a routine stop for his job in Child Protective Services, finds himself and a group of innocent customers trapped inside his stepfather's store. Action thriller, starring Tyrese Gibson, John Malkovich, Christopher Backus and Michael Jai White

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This edition focuses on a mother's role in different family units. Jacob, 20, arrives in resus with suspected internal bleeding after crashing his car into a wall at high speed, while Joan, 82, is treated for a head wound after tripping down the stairs at home, and seven-year-old Jackson has a suspected infection on his cheek. Documentary following life inside the A&E department of St George's in south-west London, focusing on patients, their families and the staff dealing with the emergencies

programme.title: The Emergency Ward
programme.desc: Registrar Dr Andrew acts fast to save a builder's lacerated arm after he put his hand through a window. Dr Michelle suspects catastrophic spinal damage in a surfer who crashed headfirst into a sandbank. Dr Amy checks for life-threatening internal injuries in a man who's been in a high-speed car accident, and doctor becomes patient when Dr David falls off his skateboard

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This episode focuses on the chances people take in life and whether a gamble ever pays off. 20-year-old Megan is rushed to St George's hospital after being involved in a high-speed car crash, and as doctors begin treatment and work to assess her injuries, it soon becomes clear how her hedonistic lifestyle has put her at risk. Meanwhile, James, who's 78, is transferred from his local hospital with suspected internal bleeding, and 40-year-old Jibraeel has lodged a cotton bud in his ear

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A man suffers serious spinal injuries after falling off a mountain bike. As doctors assess the extent of the damage, his wife recalls growing up in apartheid-era South Africa. A medical team gives emergency oxygen to an 83-year-old woman who is struggling to breathe, and a woman is rushed to A&E after her daughter accidently crashed into a lamp post

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty and Briony May Williams embark on an epic journey through the Nordic region. Starting in the Viking fjords of west Norway, they take to the seas to uncover how cod farming could be part of the future. Travelling south through Sweden's ancient landscape, they pay a special visit to a certain furniture store and learn just what makes a meatball so Swedish. They finish their journey in Finland, home to the happiest people in the world apparently, to find out what kind of food is putting a smile on their faces

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole is rather taken with a throne salvaged from an exotic nightclub and Simon O'Brien's restoration of a Victorian doll's house fails to go to plan

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien are in Northamptonshire and Coventry, where Henry discovers a rare limited edition Honda Goldwing motorbike with only 65 miles on the clock

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at Rob and Jo Smith's dog-friendly B&B in Hope Mountain, north Wales. Eyebrows are raised when self-styled 'queens of clean' Steffi Stephenson and Sarah Banks find evidence of their pet hate. During an afternoon making jam, identical twins Fernando and Emilio Tundidor reveal a competitive streak. At breakfast, Denise and Alan Newman are surprisingly demanding

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At the Beaumont Hotel in Louth, Lincolnshire, two guests are damning about the breakfast quality, so it remains to be seen how managers Steffi Stevenson and Sarah Banks will react

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to Rock House Hotel in Lynmouth, Devon, where owner Emilio Tundidor is being ably assisted by his identical twin brother Fernando

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay is at Troy House in Painswick, Gloucestershire, where owners Alan and Denise Newman hope the guests will love their rooms, but Emilio and Fernando are on the warpath

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day in the competition sees the B&B owners meet up for the last time to find out what they have been paid and settle some scores

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hotel duty manager Andrew Carruthers hosts the first dinner party from Cumbria, where he hopes to impress his fellow diners with his hotel-themed evening, complete with five-star quality food including 'early reservation fishcakes' and a 'do not disturb truffle' in an attempt to win the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Fitness fanatic Jonny Thompson hosts the second night in Cumbria, where he hopes to wow his guests with a meaty menu - and they get to see a lot more of him than they had bargained for

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Eccentric sheepdog trainer Katy Cropper hosts the third dinner party in Cumbria, where she prepares 'come a cropper' prawns al fresco, opens a bottle of Champagne with a Masai sword, and serves some potent home-made horseradish sauce. Aside from the food, a sheep-herding demonstration proves to be a hit with her guests

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Holly Venning hosts the fourth dinner party from Cumbria and the food marketing manager wants to show her love for her home county through her menu. She prepares Cumbrian scallops and chorizo, followed by a traditional Cumberland sausage - but her Penrith fudge pudding doesn't appeal to everybody

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Estate agent Wendy Tinnion helms the final event in Cumbria and decides to throw a Friday night disco for her fellow diners. However, her guests are in for a shock when they discover something unexpected about their host

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Retired professional rugby player Steve Lloyd and his partner Kathy Leather host first, at Copper Beech House in Malvern in Worcestershire. John and Della McGinn are wowed by the decor, but Phil and Sharon Hobson are less impressed by their bathroom. During an afternoon rugby training session, Mark and Lowrie Price surprise their rivals. Foodie John puts Steve to the test at breakfast, and Steve and Kathy find Phil's 'sausage feedback' hard to swallow

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to the pet-friendly Dog in a Doublet in Peterborough, where Della McGinn's passion for animals comes a close second to husband John's passion for food

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at the Torcroft Boutique B&B in Torquay, south Devon, where Phil and Sharon Hobson hope their added extras will help win over their rivals

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay is at the 600-year-old Rhydspence Inn in Herefordshire, where the medieval character proves charming, although the everyday practicalities prove painful for some

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day of competition sees the B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and to settle some scores. Copper Beech House are first to host and Steve and Kathy want Phil to swallow his synthetic sausage comment

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil Spencer catches up with two sets of serial renters who had very particular tastes, beginning with serial renters Mike and Poppy. Mike has lived in 18 homes, but Poppy wants to put down roots and they've chosen Cheshire as the right spot for their family. However, living in a high spec rental has given them great expectations for the home they'd love to own. Meanwhile, James and Hayley are looking for a rural retreat for themselves and their two dogs in Swindon and Newbury. But they are the first ones to admit that their wish list is extravagant, making Phil Spencer's job a tough one

programme.title: PopMaster TV
programme.desc: Ken Bruce returns with the music quiz, with the first heat featuring five players testing their musical knowledge as they attempt to win a place in the grand final

programme.title: Our Tiny Islands
programme.desc: On Iona, sheep farmer Joanne cares for the family's flock, but also plays an integral role in wool production on the island, resulting in a product that's unique to the island. Meanwhile, 350 miles away on Lundy Island, we meet employees of the Lundy Company who are able to live on the island by virtue of their jobs there. On North Uist, Alexander is using his skills acquired as a gardener on the mainland to reinvigorate his nine-and-a-half-acre croft

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Rich, 35, is airlifted to the hospital after being involved in a high-speed collision on the M25, and doctors are concerned when they discover he has lost feeling below his chest and can't move his legs. Meanwhile, 36-year-old Justin has had a life-threatening accident while playing football and he is put into an induced coma while a CT scan is carried out, which reveals a potentially life-threatening bleed on his brain

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A baby has a dangerously high temperature and the paramedics suspect sepsis, and a woman who is pregnant and has tested positive for Covid-19 has had a migraine for seven days. A woman has a blood pressure reading that's barely detectable. She is so ill, the paramedics are concerned that she may not survive a trip to hospital

programme.title: Our Tiny Islands
programme.desc: On Iona, sheep farmer Joanne cares for the family's flock, but also plays an integral role in wool production on the island, resulting in a product that's unique to the island. Meanwhile, 350 miles away on Lundy Island, we meet employees of the Lundy Company who are able to live on the island by virtue of their jobs there. On North Uist, Alexander is using his skills acquired as a gardener on the mainland to reinvigorate his nine-and-a-half-acre croft

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Rich, 35, is airlifted to the hospital after being involved in a high-speed collision on the M25, and doctors are concerned when they discover he has lost feeling below his chest and can't move his legs. Meanwhile, 36-year-old Justin has had a life-threatening accident while playing football and he is put into an induced coma while a CT scan is carried out, which reveals a potentially life-threatening bleed on his brain

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This edition focuses on a mother's role in different family units. Jacob, 20, arrives in resus with suspected internal bleeding after crashing his car into a wall at high speed, while Joan, 82, is treated for a head wound after tripping down the stairs at home, and seven-year-old Jackson has a suspected infection on his cheek. Documentary following life inside the A&E department of St George's in south-west London, focusing on patients, their families and the staff dealing with the emergencies

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty and Matt Tebbutt embark on an extraordinary road trip through America's Deep South. Starting on the Gulf Coast, in New Orleans, they discover how the shells from the huge number of oysters consumed locally could protect the coastline. Heading through Mississippi, Jimmy and Matt find out what makes southern sweet tea different from a British cuppa. In Alabama, Matt wants to know why they fry green tomatoes, and Jimmy uncovers exactly what grits are. Then it's off to Tennessee to visit the Jack Daniels distillery and discover the secrets of sour mash. In Louisville, where the South meets the North, Jimmy and Matt learn about fast-food consistency at Kentucky Fried Chicken HQ

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Northamptonshire in search of more upcycling prospects. Simon finds some night vision goggles and an antique snooker table that's seen better days, while Henry chooses an enormous US army truck as one of his picks

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien search for more items to fix up and sell on, visiting a Norman castle, restoring a Fiat 500 and making a table from part of Southend pier

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition starts at Chimneys B&B in Holbrook in Suffolk, where owner Carolyn Spilling, who's joined by her friend Delia Duell, hopes that her uptempo personality will be a hit with the guests. However, guests Andrea and John are unimpressed to discover that they're in self-catering. At breakfast, Italian cousins Massimo and Raf aren't keen on the home from home approach

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Neale and Julie Taylor's vegetarian B&B, Copperfields Guest House in Broadstairs, Kent. Carolyn and Delia give their room a thorough going over, while parking causes concerns for Andrea and John. The guests get competitive when they make bird boxes in the afternoon and at dinner, Neale and Julie make a revelation about a secret breakfast dish that intrigues chef Massimo

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at The George and Abbotsford in Melrose, Scotland, where Andria Gardner and John Marr aim to pull out all the stops to impress their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests spend the night at Massimo Perrulli's hotel in County Durham, where one couple have cleanliness concerns, another are missing a bath and the third have an axe to grind

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet to find out what they have been paid and settle some scores. Chimneys B&B's Carolyn and her friend Delia want some home truths from Massimo and Raf

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Sports fanatic James Cox hosts the first of the week's dinner parties in Northampton, and he is confident that his French-Italian menu will bag him the £1,000 prize. However, some of his guests - Barb Wiles, Donna Harris, Duncan Fielding and Caroline Nash - are left confused by his Vichyssoise soup starter, and the tiramisu causes one of them to choke

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mother-of-three Donna Harris hosts the second dinner party in Northampton, and hopes that three courses of pure comfort-food will be enough to secure the £1,000 prize. Donna spends almost as much time fixing her make-up as she does preparing the meal, and when guests Duncan Fielding and Caroline Nash discover a secret stash of football shirts, they realise there is more to their hostess than meets the eye

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Part-time actress Caroline Nash is the host on day three of the competition in Northampton, and she hopes that a menu based on her travels will dazzle her guests. However, the night gets off to a shaky start as Donna Harris finds a hair on the side of her goat's cheese salad, while businessman Duncan Fielding seems more interested in goats themselves than the food

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Party-loving Barb Wiles hosts the fourth dinner party in the Northampton area, and hopes her Australian-themed menu will wow her guests and win her the £1,000 prize. However, the competitors are unnerved by her interest in extraterrestrials while snooping around her bedroom, and things go from bad to worse when a seafood starter and undercooked chicken result in tears at the dinner table

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Attention-loving businessman and keen cricketer Duncan Fielding takes centre stage in hosting the final dinner party from Northampton. He creates a menu based on the story of his life - which is full of surprises and full of spice - but it proves a challenge for three of his guests, who like their food mild. His main feature is a chicken and reindeer curry, and the table is impressed to discover that he runs a community pub from the bottom of his garden

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at the White Horse Inn in Maldon, Essex, where owners Jayne and Pete Kocinoga hope their attention to detail will win over their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Barnfield B&B in St Osyth, Essex, run by Christine and Paul, where as well as offering rooms, Christine also runs spiritual retreats

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: For the third visit of the week the contestants head to The Black Swan Inn in Pickering, North Yorkshire, where husband and wife Phil and Jill Hall hope to impress their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay is at Delamere Farm near Peterborough, where proud owner Agi Chapman is sent into a spin after hearing some critical comments over breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day arrives, and the hosts of The White Horse Inn, Barnfield B&B, The Black Swan Inn and Delamere Farm all discover just how much their guests valued their services

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil Spencer catches up with two sets of discerning house-hunters, each with very strong ideas on what they wanted. The searches may have been tough - in some instances they didn't even make it inside some of the properties - but both have faced even more challenging times since. In 2012, couple Robin and Colin wanted to move outside of London, but only an architectural gem was going to do. In 2014, Lia and Andrew said they were done renting in London's popular Isle of Dogs, and wanted to buy their own home now they were starting a family

programme.title: PopMaster TV
programme.desc: Ken Bruce hosts Britain's toughest music quiz, as five more players compete in the second heat, in six rounds, plus Original PopMaster and Three in Ten. The players with the highest scores across the series will secure a place in the Grand Final

programme.title: Ancient Greece by Train with Alice Roberts
programme.desc: The historian takes to the railways of Greece and Turkey as she learns about the Ancient Greek civilisation. Her journey begins in the northern Greek city of Thessaloniki, home of Alexander the Great, and visits a museum featuring a burial chamber for ancient Greek royalty. She heads south to Delphi to see the magnificent ruins of what is possibly the greatest ancient site in the world

programme.title: Castle Secrets
programme.sub-title: Nazi Crypt, the Cannibal's Caves, Hall of Screams
programme.desc: Delving into the stories behind historical fortifications. This edition explores the depths of a ruined castle where Hitler's dark occult dealings are revealed. Plus, learning about a Scottish clifftop bastion with a long history of criminal activity and a palace, which acted as a stage for betrayals

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Three boys are admitted for very different reasons, including cyclist Ollie, who arrives with head, neck and facial injuries having been involved in a traffic accident. Cameras also follow Dominic, another 16 year old. He was recovering at home after having his tonsils out when his throat started bleeding and he had to go to hospital

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Samuel, 24, is rushed to A&E after crashing his motorbike and being thrown into the road, while two-year-old Florence is brought in by her mum and dad, who suspect that she has swallowed a coin. Seventy-year-old Clive arrives after struggling to breathe at home, but his wife Linda has a difficult time keeping him in check, as he likes to tell a joke or two. Documentary following life inside the A&E department of St George's in south-west London, focusing on patients, their families and the staff

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Twenty-five-year-old Billy arrives at St George's by helicopter after being involved in a motorcycle accident in which a van ran over his chest. The team work quickly to get him into CT to see if he has any life-changing injuries, while his girlfriend, mum and stepdad desperately try to get to the hospital. Meanwhile, 58-year-old Stephen comes into A&E with his wife and son after showing signs of a stroke, and Milly brings her two-year-old son Freddie in after he jammed his thumb in a door

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A baby has a dangerously high temperature and the paramedics suspect sepsis, and a woman who is pregnant and has tested positive for Covid-19 has had a migraine for seven days. A woman has a blood pressure reading that's barely detectable. She is so ill, the paramedics are concerned that she may not survive a trip to hospital

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Andi Oliver and Matt Tebbutt journey through the islands of Barbados, Saint Lucia and Dominica to unearth the secrets of Caribbean food. In Barbados, they learn about rum production at Mount Gay, before finding out why hunting and eating lionfish could be good for the local ecosystem. In Saint Lucia the pair discover red bananas at a local market and head to Hotel Chocolat's Rabot Estate to find out why chocolate's flavour depends on where it is grown. They take a ferry to Dominica and discover a bread made by the Kalinago people with only the cassava root. Then, the duo meet a passion fruit farmer and discover why the fruit has such an evocative name

programme.title: Our Tiny Islands
programme.desc: On Iona, sheep farmer Joanne cares for the family's flock, but also plays an integral role in wool production on the island, resulting in a product that's unique to the island. Meanwhile, 350 miles away on Lundy Island, we meet employees of the Lundy Company who are able to live on the island by virtue of their jobs there. On North Uist, Alexander is using his skills acquired as a gardener on the mainland to reinvigorate his nine-and-a-half-acre croft

programme.desc: Samuel, 24, is rushed to A&E after crashing his motorbike and being thrown into the road, while two-year-old Florence is brought in by her mum and dad, who suspect that she has swallowed a coin. Seventy-year-old Clive arrives after struggling to breathe at home, but his wife Linda has a difficult time keeping him in check, as he likes to tell a joke or two. Documentary following life inside the A&E department of St George's in south-west London, focusing on patients, their families and the staff

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Rich, 35, is airlifted to the hospital after being involved in a high-speed collision on the M25, and doctors are concerned when they discover he has lost feeling below his chest and can't move his legs. Meanwhile, 36-year-old Justin has had a life-threatening accident while playing football and he is put into an induced coma while a CT scan is carried out, which reveals a potentially life-threatening bleed on his brain

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This edition focuses on a mother's role in different family units. Jacob, 20, arrives in resus with suspected internal bleeding after crashing his car into a wall at high speed, while Joan, 82, is treated for a head wound after tripping down the stairs at home, and seven-year-old Jackson has a suspected infection on his cheek. Documentary following life inside the A&E department of St George's in south-west London, focusing on patients, their families and the staff dealing with the emergencies

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty and Matt Tebbutt embark on an extraordinary road trip through America's Deep South. Starting on the Gulf Coast, in New Orleans, they discover how the shells from the huge number of oysters consumed locally could protect the coastline. Heading through Mississippi, Jimmy and Matt find out what makes southern sweet tea different from a British cuppa. In Alabama, Matt wants to know why they fry green tomatoes, and Jimmy uncovers exactly what grits are. Then it's off to Tennessee to visit the Jack Daniels distillery and discover the secrets of sour mash. In Louisville, where the South meets the North, Jimmy and Matt learn about fast-food consistency at Kentucky Fried Chicken HQ

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Northamptonshire in search of more upcycling prospects. Simon finds some night vision goggles and an antique snooker table that's seen better days, while Henry chooses an enormous US army truck as one of his picks

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien search for more items to fix up and sell on, visiting a Norman castle, restoring a Fiat 500 and making a table from part of Southend pier

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition starts at Chimneys B&B in Holbrook in Suffolk, where owner Carolyn Spilling, who's joined by her friend Delia Duell, hopes that her uptempo personality will be a hit with the guests. However, guests Andrea and John are unimpressed to discover that they're in self-catering. At breakfast, Italian cousins Massimo and Raf aren't keen on the home from home approach

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Neale and Julie Taylor's vegetarian B&B, Copperfields Guest House in Broadstairs, Kent. Carolyn and Delia give their room a thorough going over, while parking causes concerns for Andrea and John. The guests get competitive when they make bird boxes in the afternoon and at dinner, Neale and Julie make a revelation about a secret breakfast dish that intrigues chef Massimo

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at The George and Abbotsford in Melrose, Scotland, where Andria Gardner and John Marr aim to pull out all the stops to impress their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests spend the night at Massimo Perrulli's hotel in County Durham, where one couple have cleanliness concerns, another are missing a bath and the third have an axe to grind

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet to find out what they have been paid and settle some scores. Chimneys B&B's Carolyn and her friend Delia want some home truths from Massimo and Raf

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Sports fanatic James Cox hosts the first of the week's dinner parties in Northampton, and he is confident that his French-Italian menu will bag him the £1,000 prize. However, some of his guests - Barb Wiles, Donna Harris, Duncan Fielding and Caroline Nash - are left confused by his Vichyssoise soup starter, and the tiramisu causes one of them to choke

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mother-of-three Donna Harris hosts the second dinner party in Northampton, and hopes that three courses of pure comfort-food will be enough to secure the £1,000 prize. Donna spends almost as much time fixing her make-up as she does preparing the meal, and when guests Duncan Fielding and Caroline Nash discover a secret stash of football shirts, they realise there is more to their hostess than meets the eye

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Part-time actress Caroline Nash is the host on day three of the competition in Northampton, and she hopes that a menu based on her travels will dazzle her guests. However, the night gets off to a shaky start as Donna Harris finds a hair on the side of her goat's cheese salad, while businessman Duncan Fielding seems more interested in goats themselves than the food

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Party-loving Barb Wiles hosts the fourth dinner party in the Northampton area, and hopes her Australian-themed menu will wow her guests and win her the £1,000 prize. However, the competitors are unnerved by her interest in extraterrestrials while snooping around her bedroom, and things go from bad to worse when a seafood starter and undercooked chicken result in tears at the dinner table

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Attention-loving businessman and keen cricketer Duncan Fielding takes centre stage in hosting the final dinner party from Northampton. He creates a menu based on the story of his life - which is full of surprises and full of spice - but it proves a challenge for three of his guests, who like their food mild. His main feature is a chicken and reindeer curry, and the table is impressed to discover that he runs a community pub from the bottom of his garden

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at the White Horse Inn in Maldon, Essex, where owners Jayne and Pete Kocinoga hope their attention to detail will win over their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Barnfield B&B in St Osyth, Essex, run by Christine and Paul, where as well as offering rooms, Christine also runs spiritual retreats

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: For the third visit of the week the contestants head to The Black Swan Inn in Pickering, North Yorkshire, where husband and wife Phil and Jill Hall hope to impress their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay is at Delamere Farm near Peterborough, where proud owner Agi Chapman is sent into a spin after hearing some critical comments over breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day arrives, and the hosts of The White Horse Inn, Barnfield B&B, The Black Swan Inn and Delamere Farm all discover just how much their guests valued their services

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil Spencer catches up with two sets of discerning house-hunters, each with very strong ideas on what they wanted. The searches may have been tough - in some instances they didn't even make it inside some of the properties - but both have faced even more challenging times since. In 2012, couple Robin and Colin wanted to move outside of London, but only an architectural gem was going to do. In 2014, Lia and Andrew said they were done renting in London's popular Isle of Dogs, and wanted to buy their own home now they were starting a family

programme.title: PopMaster TV
programme.desc: Ken Bruce hosts Britain's toughest music quiz, as five more players compete in the second heat, in six rounds, plus Original PopMaster and Three in Ten. The players with the highest scores across the series will secure a place in the Grand Final

programme.title: Ancient Greece by Train with Alice Roberts
programme.desc: The historian takes to the railways of Greece and Turkey as she learns about the Ancient Greek civilisation. Her journey begins in the northern Greek city of Thessaloniki, home of Alexander the Great, and visits a museum featuring a burial chamber for ancient Greek royalty. She heads south to Delphi to see the magnificent ruins of what is possibly the greatest ancient site in the world

programme.title: Castle Secrets
programme.sub-title: Nazi Crypt, the Cannibal's Caves, Hall of Screams
programme.desc: Delving into the stories behind historical fortifications. This edition explores the depths of a ruined castle where Hitler's dark occult dealings are revealed. Plus, learning about a Scottish clifftop bastion with a long history of criminal activity and a palace, which acted as a stage for betrayals

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A baby has a dangerously high temperature and the paramedics suspect sepsis, and a woman who is pregnant and has tested positive for Covid-19 has had a migraine for seven days. A woman has a blood pressure reading that's barely detectable. She is so ill, the paramedics are concerned that she may not survive a trip to hospital

programme.title: Kevin McCloud's Listed Britain
programme.desc: Kevin goes behind the scenes at Chatsworth House, and visits Castell Coch, the Marquess of Bute's Victorian gothic fantasy, complete with painted monkeys and medieval extravagance. Kevin also takes a look at the Palace of Westminster, one of the nation's treasured icons, but now badly in need of repair

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Twenty-five-year-old Billy arrives at St George's by helicopter after being involved in a motorcycle accident in which a van ran over his chest. The team work quickly to get him into CT to see if he has any life-changing injuries, while his girlfriend, mum and stepdad desperately try to get to the hospital. Meanwhile, 58-year-old Stephen comes into A&E with his wife and son after showing signs of a stroke, and Milly brings her two-year-old son Freddie in after he jammed his thumb in a door

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A baby has a dangerously high temperature and the paramedics suspect sepsis, and a woman who is pregnant and has tested positive for Covid-19 has had a migraine for seven days. programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Andi Oliver and Matt Tebbutt journey through the islands of Barbados, Saint Lucia and Dominica to unearth the secrets of Caribbean food. In Barbados, they learn about rum production at Mount Gay, before finding out why hunting and eating lionfish could be good for the local ecosystem. In Saint Lucia the pair discover red bananas at a local market and head to Hotel Chocolat's Rabot Estate to find out why chocolate's flavour depends on where it is grown. They take a ferry to Dominica and discover a bread made by the Kalinago people with only the cassava root. Then, the duo meet a passion fruit farmer and discover why the fruit has such an evocative name

Dr. Pol, Charles and Dr. Sandra bounce back and forth between the fair and farm calls, while Dr. Brenda holds down the fort at the clinic to deliver a set of bulldog puppies. When Dr. Pol and Charles get in to a bidding war over a sheep at the fair, it's anybody's guess as to who will come out on top.

Hilary Mantel, Philippa Gregory and David Starkey are among the leading authors and historians sifting through the evidence to determine why Henry had her killed

programme.title: WWII in Colour: Road to Victory
programme.desc: In September 1939 a German U-boat fires its first torpedo, setting in motion the longest continuous battle of the war. A cat and mouse game of epic proportions ensues as German U-boats and allied forces engage in a five-year battle for supremacy in the Atlantic

programme.title: World of Stonehenge
programme.desc: Neil Oliver explores the origins of Britain and its people, a story forged over thousands of years. In south Wales, he joins a team of archaeologists to examine 8,000-year-old footprints revealed beneath tidal mud banks, and abseils into a cave dating back 33,000 years. The historian also reveals how, as the last ice age receded some 11,000 years ago, Britain became an island, a fate that was sealed following a cataclysmic tsunami around 6,000 BC

programme.title: The Stuarts
programme.sub-title: And I Will Make Them One Nation
programme.desc: Clare Jackson examines the history of the Stuarts, who grappled with the chaos of three separate kingdoms, multiple religions and civil war during the 17th century. In the opening episode, she looks at James VI of Scotland's attempts to unite his country with England and Ireland and persuade his subjects to feel more British

programme.title: Impossible Peace

programme.title: Plunderer: The Life and Times of a Nazi Art Thief
programme.desc: Part one of two. Jonathan Petropoulos investigates the life of former Nazi art dealer Bruno Lohse, who became Hermann Göring's personal collector, tasked with finding desirable works of art. After the Second World War, Lohse spent a brief time in prison but later resumed his career as a dealer, often trading in art looted during the conflict

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Peter Mason from Herefordshire brings an old-fashioned ice cream maker into the Repair Shop. Once belonging to his beloved Granny Ethel, Peter hopes metal craftsman Dom can bring the sentimental kitchen curiosity back to life. David Burville takes on the challenge of restoring a Spanish barrel organ known as an organillo. Owner Carolina Urraca is hoping David can breathe new life into the instrument she loved to play when growing up with her grandmother in Spain. Audio specialist tackles the repair of Lorraine McCourt's portable record player from the 1950s. Originally a gift from her father to her mother when they were courting, the precious momento brings back memories of Lorraine's parents dancing to Nat King Cole. Finally, Malcom Jordan from Chippenham brings in a 65 year-old leather document case for Susie Fletcher to repair

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell revisit more of the programme's most memorable searches, catching up with three people whose lives have been transformed in ways they never imagined. programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: BBC world affairs editor John Simpson always thought his great-grandfather was Samuel F Cody, the Wild West entertainer who also achieved fame as the first person in Britain to conduct a powered flight. programme.desc: A profile of renowned accordion player Sharon Shannon, charting her life and career from humble beginnings in rural county Clare to sold out venues in Tokyo. The film takes in career-defining highlights including her time with the Warterboys, her record-breaking 1991 self-titled debut album, her role in the compilation LP A Woman's Heart and the unexpected global success of Galway Girl. programme.title: Beyond Paradise
programme.desc: As the harvest ends, Stella and George Ellis follow their long-held tradition of hanging a corn doll to mark its close. However, this simple ritual takes a darker turn when the doll mysteriously falls and a figure dressed as the mythical Cornman strikes. With their milk ruined and their hay set ablaze, the Ellises are pushed to the brink. Meanwhile, Martha and Humphrey embark on a new chapter as foster parents when Rosie begins her first day at Shipton Abbott Primary, while Anne takes a bold step to embrace life fully. Drama, starring Kris Marshall and Sally Bretton, with guest star Caroline Quentin

programme.title: The Walsh Sisters
programme.sub-title: It's Not the Worst Thing That's Happened This Week
programme.desc: Anna is in the hospital, reeling from events of the incident and desperate for answers. In rehab, Rachel is restless and increasingly agitated, struggling through withdrawal. Meanwhile, Maggie tells Garv she wants to start IVF, but he brushes her off, reluctantly agreeing to a rigid sex schedule

programme.title: Swindlers
programme.desc: The stories of some of Ireland's most renowned con artists and white collar criminals, told by their victims, friends and colleagues they deceived, journalists that covered the cases and the law enforcers who brought them to justice. This edition looks at international con artist, bigamist and sweet-talking criminal Julia Holmes from Tyrone

programme.title: The Korean War
programme.desc: Part one of two. Shedding new light on one of the most perilous episodes in post-war history, examining how and why its legacy - a tense stalemate - continues to this day. Beginning with 35 years of brutal Japanese occupation, a sudden and arbitrary division of the 1,000 year-old nation in 1945, followed by three more years of occupation by the USSR in the North and the US in the South

programme.title: Mayflies

Thirty years later, Tully gets back in touch with Jimmy bearing bad news, and makes a request that will test their friendship, love and loyalty to the limit. Andrea Gibb's adaptation of Andrew O'Hagan's novel, starring Tony Curran and Martin Compston

programme.desc: The former Crystal Palace chairman examines the impact of money on sport, beginning with a look at the history of the English Premier League. Jordan reveals how it was created and very nearly didn't happen, and why Manchester United are still its biggest financial superpower

programme.title: Ragdoll

programme.desc: The story of renowned artist Brian Maguire, who uses his work to tell the stories of those who have fallen by the wayside and reflect the injustices of the real world. programme.title: Ennio: The Maestro

programme.title: Record On
programme.sub-title: Record On: The Specials - A Message to You
programme.desc: A profile of the band, spanning the decades from the band's formation to later albums Encore and Protest Songs

programme.title: Classic Albums
programme.desc: The making of U2's album The Joshua Tree, which features the tracks I Still Haven't Found What I'm Looking For and With or Without You. Members of the band and producers Brian Eno and Daniel Lanois reveal stories behind the 1987 classic

programme.title: Discovering Music
programme.desc: Music critics shed fresh light on the career of award-winning American singer-songwriter Paul Simon, whose fame, influence and success began with his partnership with Art Garfunkel

programme.title: Cirque du Soleil: Varekai
programme.desc: The circus company tells the story of Icarus, whose wings melt when he flies to close to the sun, sending him crashing into the volcanic and mysterious world of Varekai

programme.title: Artist of the Year: Masterclass
programme.desc: Tutorials for budding artists, with this edition focusing on painting hands. Tai-Shan Schierenberg has tips on painting portraits

programme.title: Artist of the Year: Masterclass

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A lonely alcoholic is delighted when she bumps into an old friend from university, but he might have an ulterior motive for seeing her

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: An echo from the past comes back to haunt a ruthless executive, and his wife becomes suspicious

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: A couple driving through a rural location in the the Deep South discover that the police and judge of the town of Robertsville are more intent on extorting money from them than being honest servants of the people. With Richard Kiley and Walter Matthau

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: The fate of accused killer Lloyd Ashley depends on whether or not his lawyer Mark Robeson can prove that a gun can fire accidentally, even with the safety catch on. With Barry Sullivan and Leslie Nielsen

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of the American actress, who received Oscar nominations for her roles in Dangerous Liaisons, The Fabulous Baker Boys and Love Field

programme.title: André Rieu: Welcome to My World
programme.sub-title: André Rieu: Falling in Love Again
programme.desc: André performs his emotional first concert after the pandemic and welcomes a new soprano to the Johann Strauss family.

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: Colonel George Peregrine's comfortable life is disturbed when his wife writes a raunchy novel that becomes a best-seller and he becomes convinced she is having an affair. With Joss Ackland, Pauline Collins, Rupert Frazer and Gareth Thomas

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A surgeon saves the life of a wealthy patient and receives a wonderful gift - but his new asset proves a fearful liability. Starring John Alderton and Alison Fiske

programme.title: Musical Masterpieces
programme.desc: Myleene Klass and Errollyn Wallen explore Carmen, Georges Bizet's controversial opera. Features special performances from leading British opera company Opera North

programme.title: Classic Albums
programme.desc: The making of Black Sabbath's second album Paranoid, which was released in 1970 and helped define the sound and style of heavy metal. The programme tells the story of how Ozzy Osbourne, Tony Iommi, Geezer Butler and Bill Ward created the record, and features contributions by Phil Alexander, Geoff Barton, Henry Rollins and Jim Simpson

programme.desc: A look at the winner's journey through the competition and the work they have done along the way to claiming the title

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: When the new vicar of a church plans radical changes, conflict arises over a secret one of the locals has kept for years

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A fencing contest in London lands naive Nicholas Lillie in the middle of a bizarre adventure. Drama, with Benedict Taylor and Jim Broadbent

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: In the near future of 1965, a drone seeks escape from his dull job, and his wife's constant demands. Charles Brailing longs to chuck it all and fly down to Rio. Norman Lloyd and Marian Seldes star

programme.desc: Karen Adams' infidelity drives her husband Carl to attempt suicide by jumping from a high window ledge at a hotel, where a police officer tries to stop him. With Gary Merrill and Peter Mark Richman

programme.title: Classic Albums

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A woman joins a dating agency to try to overcome her shyness, but her plan backfires when an out-of-town solicitor tries a one-night stand

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: An American thief faces extradition from a Middle East country for a crime he did not commit. Michael Brandon stars

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: A gun shop owner sees two mobsters murdering a witness from another crime in 1927 Chicago and is afraid he will share the same fate. With Art Carney and Robert Bray

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: Lucy Pryor allows herself to be hypnotised at a house party by Miles Farnham. While under his spell, she picks up a pair of scissors and kills her husband, apparently influenced by the spirit of a woman who committed the same crime more than 100 years before. With Phyllis Thaxter and Tom Helmore

programme.title: Leonardo da Vinci: The Universal Man
programme.desc: Profile of the Renaissance polymath, examining how he was able to paint the Mona Lisa, invent the ball bearing and be the first to give a clinical description of arteriosclerosis

programme.title: Decoding Turner
programme.desc: An analysis of the life and work of JMW Turner as two enthusiasts are joined by art historians and academics to reveal the hidden codes they believe are woven into his paintings

programme.desc: A 2023 concert at the Royal Arena in the Danish capital, with Neil Tennant and Chris Lowe performing hits including West End Girls, Suburbia, Rent, Domino Dancing and It's a Sin

programme.title: Queen: From Rags to Rhapsody
programme.desc: Documentary recalling the rock band's early years, spent struggling gigging at pubs and colleges, and following their progress up to the moment they captured the UK's hearts and minds with what was to become one of the greatest songs of all time - Bohemian Rhapsody. The documentary features contributions by band members and outtakes from recording sessions

programme.desc: A profile of the artist, featuring interviews with him in London and Normandy, and footage of him at work

programme.title: Urban Secrets

He also explores the district of Clifton and visits a speakeasy-style bar

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming visits Liverpool, cutting through cliches about football and the Beatles to explore its less-celebrated treasures. Among his destinations are a 1930s banking building that has remained unaltered since closing in 2007, a well-appointed set of toilets and a recording studio hidden in a bar. He also discovers which former dictator once spent Christmas in the city

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: Maritime Inn landlady Pat gets her first full house of the season as Brixham's tourist trade picks up for the summer - but refuses to hire anyone to help her with the extra work. Powerboat instructor Mike Weatherbee tries to enforce the laws of the sea, carpet-cleaner Dave Satchwill prepares to lead the town's popular ghost tour, and lone fisherman Dave Brown reveals his unusual methods

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: August arrives and the fishermen of Brixham spend time ashore with their families as their boats are repaired - but summer proves costly for Jan, who loses £1,000 for every day his vessel is out of action. Apprentice deckhand Logan has to decide whether a life at sea is the right choice for him and his family, and seafood seller Jenny receives a surprise on her 65th birthday

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: A prison riot breaks out and two inmates are killed. Richard Belzer stars

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming discovers more little-known facts on a journey between Smithfields and Spitalfields, two of London's most famous markets. He begins with a fry-up breakfast before visiting the remains of a Roman amphitheatre and investigating a mysterious secret in a Liverpool Street hotel, as well as meeting former EastEnders star Larry Lamb (Archie Mitchell)

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming explores Newcastle, discovering strange sounds by the river and finding out how traditional culture is being reinterpreted in the Byker district. He goes behind the scenes at Newcastle United with local football hero Peter Beardsley, learns about the history of adult comic Viz from former editor Simon Donald and accompanies comedienne Sarah Millican to her favourite part of the city

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor returns to explore more of the world's most luxurious hotels and begins in Miami's South Beach, home to some of the most hedonistic and extravagant establishments in America. At the Fontainebleau, he meets a man with eye-opening stories about Frank Sinatra's regular visits and gets a shock when he discovers how much it costs to hire a booth in the night club. Richard also gatecrashes swimwear fashion week at the Raleigh Hotel, where he speaks to the event manager about the many celebrity parties he has organised

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: In this edition the actor explores the luxury hotels of Hong Kong. Home to more skyscrapers than any other city, he first checks in to the Mandarin in the financial district, before experiencing the Four Seasons, the only hotel with two Michelin three-star resturants, as well as the timeless Peninsula in Kowloon, which even provides a Rolls-Royce airport taxi service. He ends his stay at the Ritz-Carlton, home to the world's highest swimming pool

programme.title: Deadwood

programme.desc: Alan Cumming discovers more little-known facts on a journey between Smithfields and Spitalfields, two of London's most famous markets. He begins with a fry-up breakfast before visiting the remains of a Roman amphitheatre and investigating a mysterious secret in a Liverpool Street hotel, as well as meeting former EastEnders star Larry Lamb (Archie Mitchell)

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming explores Newcastle, discovering strange sounds by the river and finding out how traditional culture is being reinterpreted in the Byker district. He goes behind the scenes at Newcastle United with local football hero Peter Beardsley, learns about the history of adult comic Viz from former editor Simon Donald and accompanies comedienne Sarah Millican to her favourite part of the city

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor returns to explore more of the world's most luxurious hotels and begins in Miami's South Beach, home to some of the most hedonistic and extravagant establishments in America. At the Fontainebleau, he meets a man with eye-opening stories about Frank Sinatra's regular visits and gets a shock when he discovers how much it costs to hire a booth in the night club. Richard also gatecrashes swimwear fashion week at the Raleigh Hotel, where he speaks to the event manager about the many celebrity parties he has organised

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: In this edition the actor explores the luxury hotels of Hong Kong. Home to more skyscrapers than any other city, he first checks in to the Mandarin in the financial district, before experiencing the Four Seasons, the only hotel with two Michelin three-star resturants, as well as the timeless Peninsula in Kowloon, which even provides a Rolls-Royce airport taxi service. He ends his stay at the Ritz-Carlton, home to the world's highest swimming pool

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Lewis tackles a drug-related homicide with a little help from a female detective in narcotics. Meanwhile, Frank misses passing his test by four points. Gritty police drama, following the lives of the detective division on the streets of Baltimore, starring Richard Belzer

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: An FBI agent interviews the squad about Kellerman's behaviour. Gritty police drama, starring Richard Belzer

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: The town is left reeling in the wake of William's accident, and Hostetler and the General flee in fear of retribution once word gets out that the horse was in their care. Meanwhile, Swearengen finally receives word from Yankton. Drama, starring Ian McShane, Brad Dourif and Timothy Olyphant

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: The residents of Deadwood face a sad day when William's funeral is held, and Seth Bullock and his wife prepare to leave the town following their son's tragic death. Meanwhile, Alma offers her answer to Ellsworth. Timothy Olyphant and Anna Gunn star

programme.title: Billions
programme.desc: With victory in sight for his bank, Axe plots to secure his deposits by poaching from Prince. Meanwhile, Taylor looks to enlist an old foe, and Wags prepares for a big day

programme.title: Billions
programme.desc: In the series finale, Chuck, Axe and Prince attempt to outsmart each other, while Wendy struggles to deal with issues in her personal life

programme.title: My Brilliant Friend
programme.desc: After her daughter is born, Elena is consumed with jealousy when Nino and Lila step in to take Immacolata to the hospital. In Italian

programme.title: The New Pope
programme.desc: The terrorist threat becomes increasingly menacing. Meanwhile, Assente's policies begin to undermine the Church's credibility

programme.desc: Albie asks Dominic for a karmic payment to help Lucia, Tanya grows wary of Quentin's motives, Ethan confronts Cam, and Valentina gives Mia a chance

programme.title: Task
programme.desc: After scaling back his role in the FBI and still reeling from a family tragedy, Special Agent Tom Brandis reluctantly returns to the field to head a new, relatively inexperienced team to investigate a string of robberies in Philadelphia. Following his brother's death, Robbie Prendergrast earns extra cash for his family by ripping off drug dealers - but a bad tip upends his delicate balance. Crime drama, starring Mark Ruffalo

programme.title: Prisoner
programme.desc: While being tracked down, Amber and Tibor turn the tables on Nina and hitch a ride to London in the back of a lorry, unaware that their driver has sold them out. Action thriller, starring Tahar Rahim and Izuka Hoyle

programme.title: Atomic
programme.desc: In Marrakesh, Max and JJ prepare for the uranium exchange with the cartel, all under the watchful eye of Cassie

programme.title: The White Lotus
programme.desc: Albie asks Dominic for a karmic payment to help Lucia, Tanya grows wary of Quentin's motives, Ethan confronts Cam, and Valentina gives Mia a chance

programme.title: Task
programme.desc: As Robbie and Cliff try to find a potential buyer, Tom and Grasso level with the Dark Hearts, and Aleah and Lizzie question Peaches' former co-workers. Later, a raid leads Tom to a shocking revelation

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Lewis tackles a drug-related homicide with a little help from a female detective in narcotics. Meanwhile, Frank misses passing his test by four points. Gritty police drama, following the lives of the detective division on the streets of Baltimore, starring Richard Belzer

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: An FBI agent interviews the squad about Kellerman's behaviour. Gritty police drama, starring Richard Belzer

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: A newspaper features a story about Kellerman and the other members of the team. Richard Belzer and Reed Diamond star

programme.title: Gremlins 2: The New Batch (1990)
programme.desc: Cute furball Gizmo is reunited with his human friends Billy and Kate, who now work in the New York offices of an eccentric tycoon. However, it is not long before Gizmo has spawned a new generation of murderous carnivores, who quickly overrun the building - and a genetics lab where they produce even more startling mutations. Joe Dante's comedy sequel, starring Zach Galligan, Phoebe Cates, John Glover and Christopher Lee

programme.title: Tremors (1990)
programme.desc: A dull American town in the middle of the desert is suddenly attacked by huge man-eating subterranean creatures. Luckily, two dim-witted but brave handymen step into the breach to lead a ragtag bunch of locals and a mismatched pair of survivalists in the battle against the gruesome worm-like monsters. Comedy horror, with Kevin Bacon, Fred Ward, Finn Carter, Michael Gross, Reba McEntire and Bobby Jacoby

programme.title: Blades of Glory (2007)
programme.desc: A bad-boy figure skater and his effeminate arch-enemy are banned from the sport after their intense rivalry results in a brawl. However, the pair find a loophole in the regulations allowing them to enter the world championships as the first ever same-sex pair - if they can put aside their differences long enough to compete. Comedy, starring Will Ferrell, Jon Heder, Will Arnett and Amy Poehler

programme.title: The 40 Year-Old Virgin (2005)
programme.desc: A middle-aged shop worker reveals he has never been intimate with a woman, which prompts his shocked colleagues to set him up with a series of potentially passionate dates. When he falls for a single mother, his friends think he is right on course for a lusty liaison, but his new partner is not ready for a physical relationship. Judd Apatow's comedy, starring Steve Carell, Catherine Keener and Paul Rudd

programme.title: Mean Girls (2004)
programme.desc: A teenager is surprised when she is befriended by the girls in the most popular clique at her new school. However, a dating mishap leads to trouble and her supposed friends start to turn on her. Urged on by other pals outside their circle, the newcomer resolves to bring down the self-appointed queen of campus - only to end up adopting some of her rival's crueller characteristics. Comedy, with Lindsay Lohan, Rachel McAdams, Tina Fey and Lacey Chabert

programme.title: Napoleon Dynamite (2004)
programme.desc: A socially inept high-school student and his lonely unemployed brother find their eccentric home life thrown into upheaval when their meddling uncle comes to stay. The teenage oddball tries to make the best of his changing circumstances, while putting all his skills to the task of helping his new best friend win the class presidency. Comedy, starring Jon Heder, Efren Ramirez and Haylie Duff

programme.title: Blades of Glory (2007)
programme.desc: A bad-boy figure skater and his effeminate arch-enemy are banned from the sport after their intense rivalry results in a brawl. However, the pair find a loophole in the regulations allowing them to enter the world championships as the first ever same-sex pair - if they can put aside their differences long enough to compete.
