programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: As part of the 2013 appeal, Nick Knowles and the team head to Peterborough to help Children in Need-funded organisation Little Miracles, which supports youngsters with disabilities and life-limiting illnesses and their families. However, whereas Mark, Chris, Julian and Billy normally renovate an existing property, for the first time they are knocking down a building and reconstructing it from the ground up - making it worth almost £1million. With just nine days to complete the job, they'll need help - and lots of it. Luckily, tradespeople turn out in force - from both the local area and across the country - giving their time for free

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: In the summer of 2015, West Sussex wife and mother-of-four Amanda fell off her bike while training for an endurance contest, breaking 11 bones, puncturing a lung, and snapping her collarbone and back. Paralysed, she spent the next six months at Stoke Mandeville's spinal unit, while her builder husband Vic looked after the children - but determined to compete again, she returned home in a wheelchair. However, the family home is no longer suitable for Amanda - so that's where Nick Knowles and the rest of the DIY SOS team come in, strengthened by a number of local tradesmen

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the DIY SOS team transform the home of a police officer who was a victim of the Westminster Bridge terrorist attack in March 2017, suffering horrific injuries which left him wheelchair bound. Months after the attack took place, Kris still resides in hospital as his north London home is inaccessible for him, so the team and hundreds of volunteers work to get Kris living back at home to his family

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Rescue Labrador Tom needs work with a serious wound on one of his front paws, and four-year-old French bulldog called Otis has suffered an accident while being chased by another dog. Professor Noel Fitzpatrick diagnoses a spinal problem high up in Otis's neck that requires surgery. Meanwhile, Bailey, a 10-year-old cocker spaniel, has quite a lot of pain in one of his hind limbs, but his owner wants to avoid surgery, and Noel needs to perform specialist imaging before he can offer any solutions

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: As the festive season approaches at Fitzpatrick Referrals, Professor Noel and his team treat three special patients. A one-year-old crossbreed puppy rescued by a charity from Romania is in need of complex surgery, with no guarantee of success. An eight-year-old Dalmatian needs work on its spinal cord, and a Labrador from Switzerland is having trouble walking. Noel's investigations reveal problems with both shoulder and elbow, an unusual combination that requires dramatic intervention

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: As part of the 2013 appeal, Nick Knowles and the team head to Peterborough to help Children in Need-funded organisation Little Miracles, which supports youngsters with disabilities and life-limiting illnesses and their families. programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing and TOWIE star Pete Wicks immerses himself in the heart-warming and emotional world of dog care at Basildon's Dogs Trust rehoming centre. It's Pete's first day at Dogs Trust, and he meets a pug facing a worrying surgery and pregnant dog who was seized at the border

programme.title: Untold: The Disappearance of Shannon Matthews
programme.desc: In February 2008, nine-year-old Shannon Matthews vanishes on her way home from school, triggering one of the highest-profile missing person investigations ever seen in the UK. Her mother is distraught and the community rallies in support, but no trace of the child can be found

programme.title: Untold: The Disappearance of Shannon Matthews
programme.desc: In February 2008, nine-year-old Shannon Matthews vanishes on her way home from school, triggering one of the highest-profile missing person investigations ever seen in the UK. With increasing alarm sweeping through Shannon's hometown and Britain at large, the search for the missing nine-year-old grows in scale

programme.title: Lies, Deceit and Betrayal
programme.desc: Two couples trying to secure their retirement each find themselves at the centre of an elaborate investment scam, giving thousands of pounds to someone who doesn't seem to exist

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Journalist and broadcaster Piers Morgan travels across the USA to meet some of America's most notorious female killers in a quest to discover what drives women to kill. He travels to Gatesville, Texas, to visit Erin Caffey, who masterminded the murder of her entire family at the age of 16, and will remain behind bars until she is almost 60. Piers finds out what made a typical teenager conspire with her boyfriend to kill her mum, dad, and two younger brothers, before meeting the only survivor of the tragedy, Erin's father Terry

programme.title: Deadly Waters with Captain Lee
programme.desc: When a Florida couple goes missing, the discovery of a grisly bait freezer leads investigators to a surprise informant who claims the truth is hidden at sea

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: The Death Cleaners meet Sue, who helped build a lesbian-safe section of Kansas City in the 80s and hasn't been able to tidy up since the love of her life died

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: In Dudley, Ash and Jamie blue light to a frightened young woman with severe chest pains. Rachel and Mick are called to assist the police with a prisoner in Stoke

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Dave heads to Bolton to pick up a classic Italian sports car - a Lancia Fulvia 1.3 Sport styled by Milan coachbuilder Zagato

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: The Meldrews broaden their cultural horizons with a visit to the BBC in the company of a comatose window cleaner they think is Mrs Warboys' cousin. Later, Victor makes a fresh start with a new job - but soon blots his copybook with a characteristic outburst. Comedy, starring Richard Wilson

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.sub-title: Some Day We'll Laugh About This
programme.desc: Terry carries out some home improvements for Bob and Thelma while they are on holiday. However, he finds himself sidetracked by the attentions of their attractive neighbour Sandra. Classic comedy, starring James Bolam and Rodney Bewes. First shown in 1974

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Bob visits the hospital after injuring his foot and his misery is compounded when he has a run-in with Terry. Comedy, with James Bolam and Rodney Bewes

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Bob bids his wife farewell and sets off on a boys-only fishing trip with Terry - but guilt soon gets the better of him. Comedy, starring Rodney Bewes, James Bolam and Brigit Forsyth

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James looks at some of the advances in motoring that never caught on, revealing how the petrol engine came to dominate in the 20th century, while concepts for jet engines and steam power fell by the wayside. He also drives an amphibious car and an electric scooter, and takes a ride in the world's fastest driverless car

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Aspiring makers are given the opportunity to turn their hobbies and skills into money-making businesses by joining presenter Dom Chinea at a life-changing training camp in the grounds of a arts-and-crafts house in Leicestershire. In the first episode, Morgan's furniture is beautifully crafted, but he lacks the confidence to really sell it. Cath's life-sized fantastical willow woven creatures are unique - but are they saleable?

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: A Cold War Soviet classic goes under the hammer. Built like a tank, the Moskvitch gets few admirers for style, but finds a loving home with a fan of Eastern Bloc classics

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek Mathewson collects a Jaguar Mark 2 and explains why they made such poor getaway cars for 1960s bank robbers. A customer considers a Morris Marina as a Christmas present

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: A look at the early stages of genocide at the camp, examining the role of commander Rudolf Hoess in the process. Despite later claiming he was only following orders, Hoess was instrumental in the development of Auschwitz for mass murder, arranging for Soviet prisoners to be brought in as construction workers and scouring Europe for more victims to kill. Featuring contributions from survivors

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: Dramatised documentary examining how Hitler and the Nazi party rose to power in Germany during the 1930s, revealing the greed, manipulation and frightening underestimation of Adolf Hitler's ambition. In 1930, Germany was a liberal democracy, with elections, parliament and the rule of law, but just four years later its leader was a dictator and its government is in the hands of murderers. Leading historians and experts take viewers inside the minds of those hellbent on gaining power and those who tried to stop them. First broadcast in 1997

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the second programme of the series from the Spa Royal Hall in Bridlington, East Yorkshire. Items of interest include a set of rare buttons commemorating the Battle of Quebec in 1759 and artefacts excavated from the wreck of HMS Falcon. Plus, a first-hand account of life as a Japanese prisoner of war

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Oxburgh Hall in Norfolk, where they assess finds including family heirlooms that honour the men of the Pathfinder Squadron who fought in the Second World War. Plus, a 300-year-old treasure box packed with surprises and a set of discarded postcards

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Dave heads to West Yorkshire to collect a rare 1926 Dodge bus. Derek gets his hands on a rare Vauxhall Victor VX 90 police car that is expected to sell for more than a few coppers

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Dave heads to Bolton to pick up a classic Italian sports car - a Lancia Fulvia 1.3 Sport styled by Milan coachbuilder Zagato

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming begins a new canal adventure in Wales and encounters towering aqueducts and stunning scenery along the way

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming's journey on the Llangollen Canal takes an unexpected turn when his boat gets stuck in silt and a passing hire boat comes to the rescue

programme.title: Operation Grand Canyon with Dan Snow
programme.desc: Part one of two. The broadcaster joins a team of historians and geologists to recreate a pioneering 280-mile journey through the natural wonder that was led by explorer John Wesley Powell in 1869. Following in Powell's footsteps in the stunning yet challenging north Arizona landscape, Snow and the crew take on the area's rapids in antique wooden boats as they learn about the region's geological and Native American past

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Dave heads to West Yorkshire to collect a rare 1926 Dodge bus. Derek gets his hands on a rare Vauxhall Victor VX 90 police car that is expected to sell for more than a few coppers

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: The Mathewsons take in a 1982 Audi Quattro and a Sunbeam Alpine. Plus, Derek takes delivery of a beautiful green Renault van, previously used as a bread van in Paris

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Gus and Will want to buy a classic Rover, but have to go for a Honda-based 800 coupe. At only £650 it seems too good to be true - and when they turn up, they aren't completely sure whether to proceed

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien attempt to restore items including an old cheese press, and a 1980s motorbike almost gets the better of Henry and his restorer Guy

programme.title: Trawlermen: Hunting the Catch
programme.desc: Storm Arwen hits the UK and the skippers have tough decisions to make. Boats start to roll in the North Sea, putting Victory Rose and Our Lass at risk of collision. Skipper Darren has just three days of crabbing time before the storm pushes the boat back to the harbour and George Jack considers venturing in to the storm in search of a new species of prawn

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: After worrying her autism would stop her developing her skills, Belinda has become a talented glassblower with the potential to turn her art into a money-making business. Paul and Becky have a passion for upcycling furniture and are hoping to turn their hobby into a day job. Dom Chinea and his team of experts meets all three makers and helps them to make their aspirations a reality

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Labourer Mike has a passion for turning wooden bowls but feels held back by his perfectionist attitude to his creations. During the pandemic Sarah quit her job and took up pottery, but has been struggling to make enough money from it to live on. With the help and advice of Dom Chinea and his team of experts, Mike and Sarah try their best to create smooth running businesses out of their crafts and turn their hobbies into a day job

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: The team is under pressure with a record number of cars to auction, but Derek finds room for one more - a replica AC Cobra. A couple are reunited with their Volvo breakdown truck

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: A classic car fan with a love of the Jaguar E-Type reluctantly sends his dream car to auction, hoping to buy a motorhome with the money. Two Ford Escorts get Dave's pulse racing

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: Examining the complex system the Nazis set up to implement the extermination of the Jewish race throughout Europe from 1942, with Auschwitz as the hub. The programme reveals that at other camps, including Treblinka, Belzec and Sobibor, rival commanders participated in the genocide with enthusiasm and shared ideas for the best method of mass murder

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.sub-title: The First Six Months in Power
programme.desc: It is 1933 and Adolf Hitler is the chancellor of Germany, but he does not have absolute power as there is still a democratic parliament, a head of state and the rule of law. Hitler calls a general election to increase Nazi representation, but a fire in the Reichstag building is blamed on communists - prompting thousands of arrests of left-wingers. Hitler then calls on President von Hindenburg to act and a decree is passed giving the Nazis emergency powers to ban free speech and the right to protest

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team look back at the series and choose their favourite moments. The programme features updates on items of high value, including footage from an auction in which a £1 boot-sale purchase became the best investment in Roadshow history

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team value items at Jersey's Samares Manor, where the experts discover a variety of items with international origins. Artefacts examined include a lemon squeezer said to have been owned by Marilyn Monroe, and a forensics test is carried out to determine the value of a gold bangle

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek rubs shoulders with pop music royalty when he picks up a Morgan sports car, while a Second World War ambulance arrives at Thornton le Dale

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: The Mathewsons take in a 1982 Audi Quattro and a Sunbeam Alpine. Plus, Derek takes delivery of a beautiful green Renault van, previously used as a bread van in Paris

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming explores the English canal network, beginning with a journey from Shardlow to Etruria Junction, Stoke on Trent, exploring the Trent and Mersey Canal. However, not everything goes to plan, and he will have to deal with a tricky issue with his propeller as well as navigating the complicated locks on the Trent and Mersey Canal

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming explores life on the English canal network. A low canal tunnel in Staffordshire proves to be problematic and Robbie helps out a stranded boater

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek checks out an abandoned 1985 Renault 4 and an MGB Roadster that hasn't seen the light of day for 36 years, and Jack heads to Rotherham to pick up a pair of rare Bond Bugs.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team seek out a sturdy, reliable 4x4 to tackle the rugged Yorkshire landscape, but get side-tracked into a slightly rusty Subaru Impreza Turbo 2000

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek rubs shoulders with pop music royalty when he picks up a Morgan sports car, while a Second World War ambulance arrives at Thornton le Dale

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: There's a surprise for Derek in Hull - an MGSA sports car that once had a tree growing through it. Plus, in North Wales, Alan Holmes is selling his son's Ford Escort RS 1600i

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Gus has his eye on a Citroen XM that looks amazing - but it's in France. If they can double their money, it'll be worth the trip, but the car has some big problems. Will isn't sure, but the engineering challenges win him over

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Max McMurdo restore and upcycle 2CV headlamps, a children's rocking horse, a Victorian streetlamp and a high chair

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter examines how the car industry in Germany and Japan blossomed following the Second World War, becoming manufacturing giants that far outstripped the Allied nations that had defeated them. He test drives some of the vehicles Germany and Japan produced during the post-war period, as well as an assortment of other classic cars, including E-Types and Mustangs. He also reveals the sad tale of the Austin Allegro, a vehicle that had a devastating impact on the British car industry

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.sub-title: Some Day We'll Laugh About This
programme.desc: Terry carries out some home improvements for Bob and Thelma while they are on holiday. However, he finds himself sidetracked by the attentions of their attractive neighbour Sandra. Classic comedy, starring James Bolam and Rodney Bewes. First shown in 1974

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Bob visits the hospital after injuring his foot and his misery is compounded when he has a run-in with Terry. Comedy, with James Bolam and Rodney Bewes

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Bob bids his wife farewell and sets off on a boys-only fishing trip with Terry - but guilt soon gets the better of him. Comedy, starring Rodney Bewes, James Bolam and Brigit Forsyth

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James looks at some of the advances in motoring that never caught on, revealing how the petrol engine came to dominate in the 20th century, while concepts for jet engines and steam power fell by the wayside. He also drives an amphibious car and an electric scooter, and takes a ride in the world's fastest driverless car

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Aspiring makers are given the opportunity to turn their hobbies and skills into money-making businesses by joining presenter Dom Chinea at a life-changing training camp in the grounds of a arts-and-crafts house in Leicestershire. In the first episode, Morgan's furniture is beautifully crafted, but he lacks the confidence to really sell it. Cath's life-sized fantastical willow woven creatures are unique - but are they saleable?

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: A Cold War Soviet classic goes under the hammer. Built like a tank, the Moskvitch gets few admirers for style, but finds a loving home with a fan of Eastern Bloc classics

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek Mathewson collects a Jaguar Mark 2 and explains why they made such poor getaway cars for 1960s bank robbers. A customer considers a Morris Marina as a Christmas present

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: A look at the early stages of genocide at the camp, examining the role of commander Rudolf Hoess in the process. Despite later claiming he was only following orders, Hoess was instrumental in the development of Auschwitz for mass murder, arranging for Soviet prisoners to be brought in as construction workers and scouring Europe for more victims to kill. Featuring contributions from survivors

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: Dramatised documentary examining how Hitler and the Nazi party rose to power in Germany during the 1930s, revealing the greed, manipulation and frightening underestimation of Adolf Hitler's ambition. In 1930, Germany was a liberal democracy, with elections, parliament and the rule of law, but just four years later its leader was a dictator and its government is in the hands of murderers. Leading historians and experts take viewers inside the minds of those hellbent on gaining power and those who tried to stop them. First broadcast in 1997

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the second programme of the series from the Spa Royal Hall in Bridlington, East Yorkshire. Items of interest include a set of rare buttons commemorating the Battle of Quebec in 1759 and artefacts excavated from the wreck of HMS Falcon. Plus, a first-hand account of life as a Japanese prisoner of war

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Oxburgh Hall in Norfolk, where they assess finds including family heirlooms that honour the men of the Pathfinder Squadron who fought in the Second World War. Plus, a 300-year-old treasure box packed with surprises and a set of discarded postcards

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Dave heads to West Yorkshire to collect a rare 1926 Dodge bus. Derek gets his hands on a rare Vauxhall Victor VX 90 police car that is expected to sell for more than a few coppers

programme.title: Operation Grand Canyon with Dan Snow
programme.desc: Part one of two. The broadcaster joins a team of historians and geologists to recreate a pioneering 280-mile journey through the natural wonder that was led by explorer John Wesley Powell in 1869. Following in Powell's footsteps in the stunning yet challenging north Arizona landscape, Snow and the crew take on the area's rapids in antique wooden boats as they learn about the region's geological and Native American past

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Labourer Mike has a passion for turning wooden bowls but feels held back by his perfectionist attitude to his creations. programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: The team is under pressure with a record number of cars to auction, but Derek finds room for one more - a replica AC Cobra. A couple are reunited with their Volvo breakdown truck

programme.title: Bangers & Cash

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: Examining the complex system the Nazis set up to implement the extermination of the Jewish race throughout Europe from 1942, with Auschwitz as the hub. The programme reveals that at other camps, including Treblinka, Belzec and Sobibor, rival commanders participated in the genocide with enthusiasm and shared ideas for the best method of mass murder

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.sub-title: The First Six Months in Power
programme.desc: It is 1933 and Adolf Hitler is the chancellor of Germany, but he does not have absolute power as there is still a democratic parliament, a head of state and the rule of law. Hitler calls a general election to increase Nazi representation, but a fire in the Reichstag building is blamed on communists - prompting thousands of arrests of left-wingers. Hitler then calls on President von Hindenburg to act and a decree is passed giving the Nazis emergency powers to ban free speech and the right to protest

programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team look back at the series and choose their favourite moments. The programme features updates on items of high value, including footage from an auction in which a £1 boot-sale purchase became the best investment in Roadshow history

programme.desc: The villagers fear for a boy's life when he disappears while exploring the gardens of a derelict house with his brother and sister. Wetherby learns he has fallen down an old well and a media circus descends on the scene as firemen begin their rescue attempt. However, panic ensues when shots are fired from the house. Meanwhile, a troublesome celebrity journalist goes in search of local gossip

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A prominent bank manager is almost killed by a hit-and-run driver just before he is due to set sail on the luxury liner Queen Mary. Convinced his life is in danger, he persuades Poirot to accompany him - and his valuable shipment of liberty bonds - to New York. But the Belgian sleuth's main concern is his own terrible sea-sickness. David Suchet stars

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Presenter Helen Skelton and expert garden designer Danny Clarke head to Bradford to breathe new life into a thoroughly overgrown garden lost in time. The aim is that they will get the job completed in just one day for owner Beverley and her family

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare the perfect meal for a special night in, with a starter of scallops served with crispy bacon and a cauliflower puree. The main course is roast chicken stuffed with truffle butter with dauphinoise potatoes and charred cabbage, followed by and a chocolate and almond tart with amaretti biscuits for dessert

programme.title: Peter Andre's 60 Minute Makeover
programme.desc: Alice Lee enlists the help of Peter and interior designer Leah Hughes to revamp her mother's home in Warlingham, Surrey, after she suffered an upsetting burglary. They tackle the living room and conservatory and turn the spaces into fun, feminine and chic rooms

programme.title: Alex Polizzi's Secret Italy
programme.desc: Alex travels to the south of the country, taking in the cities of Venice, Milan, Rome and the Amalfi Coast, before unearthing hidden tourist gems in Puglia. Her secret guide revisits some of her fondest memories of this beautiful Mediterranean land, including the childhood holidays she spent in the foothills of the capital and the places she worked in her youth

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick tries to keep the peace during a feud between neighbours, and Greengrass thinks his luck has changed for the better when he wins first prize in a raffle

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The Tesla automotive manufacturing facility, where the world's strongest robots produce 2,000 cars a week. Featuring a comparison with the world's first production line

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Ruth Watson advises Keith and Val Morris on ways to revamp their failing hotel and conference centre the Hanmer Arms, which is losing money despite being large and well-equipped. She explains why the lack of a full-time receptionist and permanent head chef mean problems with continuity - and urges the reluctant and negative owners to boost their dwindling bookings by attracting corporate clients

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Robert investigates a noise in the barn while working on the farm and is attacked from behind and knocked unconscious, while Manpreet lets some news slip

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sam shows Roy a tracking device he found in his school bag, Cassie gets distracted while childminding, Kit and Lisa's investigation stalls, and Ben suffers a meltdown at the end of the week from hell

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Dr Weatherill and Sister Brigid take the Ormerod children on a family day out to Peasholm Park to see a naval re-enactment show, but the trip ends in disaster. Meanwhile, Dr Ellis risks getting too involved with a patient who is in trouble with the law, and Lizzie sets out to improve her career prospects. Starring Amy Robbins, Linda Armstrong, Neil McDermott and Michelle Hardwick

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As plans to build a new police station lead Morse to question his place in the force, he investigates the case of Tommy Cork, a boy from a broken home who has disappeared. Meanwhile, the body of disreputable journalist Eric Patterson has been found on railway tracks in mysterious circumstances. He was last seen embroiled in an argument with a council official about a company preparing to redevelop the site of Blenheim Vale, a disused correctional facility for boys. When a petty criminal with a connection to Blenheim absconds from an open prison, it sets off a chain reaction of events that threaten to expose horrors of the past. Did missing Tommy witness something that cost him his life?

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A petty thief makes several attacks in the Aidensfield area, including terrifying Gina and injuring Maggie. At least, a Bob Dylan look a like arrives and entertains everyone with his music

programme.title: Cold Feet
programme.desc: David's overbearing influence threatens to spoil the millennium celebrations on Holy Island and motivates Pete and Adam to set sail on a boat trip to the mainland. Meanwhile, Antonio recovers enough to return to work, but he is disappointed with his assignment. Chicago Fire spin-off, starring Jason Beghe and Jon Seda

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A booby-trapped cuckoo clock kills a guest at an engagement party, sending the detectives through a maze of family secrets. Detective drama set in a picturesque rural county where death lurks around every corner. Mystery drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, with Nick Hendrix, Annette Badland and Fiona Dolman

programme.title: Showtrial
programme.desc: DI Cassidy desperately tries to gather enough evidence to convince the CPS to charge Talitha, but new information concerning Troy's whereabouts threatens to derail the case. Cleo and Heidi meet with Damian and consider striking a deal, but it will mean Talitha having to reconcile with her father. Brian starts looking into the history of Talitha and Hannah's friendship, and the police track down a third suspect, who tells them a different version of what happened the night of the ball

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: Harry is shipped back to Manchester due to concerns about his mental health, and is furious at the implication he is doomed to follow in his father's footsteps. As he returns home, Kasia prepares to leave for Europe on an important mission. Henriette returns home have smuggled David out of France, and Marga plots to flee for Berlin. Rajib encounters a German patrol and tries to negotiate a ceasefire

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dawson helps the officers investigate antiques dealer Mr Brigstocke, who is accused of selling stolen goods. Mason and Wetherby stake out the shop to catch him red-handed, but the situation becomes life-threatening when Brigstocke sets fire to it. Meanwhile, prejudices are stirred by the arrival of some Taoist monks and tragedy strikes local troublemakers Julie and Eddie Tinniswood. With Clare Wille and Joe McFadden

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth is asked to vet potential suitors vying for the hand of an Australian heiress and discreetly accompanies her on a rail journey aboard the Plymouth Express, only to find himself investigating a jewel theft. Mystery, with David Suchet and Julian Wadham

programme.desc: The young detective is suspended from duty following a recent case. The victim turns out to be the estranged son of Steph and Rob Bayliss who are left crippled by his death, having lost him once already when he abandoned them to seek out his biological father, Thomas Walken, who just happens to run a salmon farm on the spot where the body was found. programme.title: The Real Housewives of New Jersey
programme.desc: Teresa takes the opportunity to mend fences with Margaret when Melissa throws an '80s-inspired roller skating party

programme.title: The Real Housewives of New Jersey
programme.desc: Teresa kicks her wedding planning into high gear as Danielle hosts a mozzarella-making party to bring the girls together

programme.title: Genius Game
programme.desc: With £32,000 in the prize pot the players face their sixth Main Match, Diamond Dilemma, a game in which the contestants are randomly split into two teams and need to work together to win as a team but they need to put themselves first to stay safe as an individual

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Aaron, Eleanor, Jacqui and Dan vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Debbie, Ian, Joe, and Kirsty pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Riddiculous
programme.desc: Ranvir Singh hosts as Suli and Viha, Louise and Jess, and Joe and Rob take on Riddlemaster Henry Lewis as they attempt to answer general knowledge questions and confusing conundrums, with only one team making it through to the high-stakes final with a chance to win a cash prize

programme.title: Moneyball
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ian Wright, in which four contestants step up and take on The Launcher - a massive money-making machine - in the studio. The first four to take part are Holly, Dom, Steven and Ben. Can Holly win big and take her kids on the holiday of a lifetime? Can Steven walk away with what he needs for an amazing wedding? And can Ben win enough to fulfil his dream of becoming an adoptive dad?

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Business manager Kieran from Wiltshire puts his faith in the fashion choices of Harry, Aaron and Connor, who have to pick an outfit for him

programme.title: Storage Hoarders
programme.desc: Aggie MacKenzie meets a man whose obsession has taken over every inch of his home. She also helps a woman who has decided it is time for a clear-out after storing away countless belongings for the past 23 years

programme.title: Paddington Station 24/7
programme.desc: A driver raises the alarm to report his train has struck something or someone on the line, and bad weather causes severe disruption when a tree falls onto the tracks and blocks the line

programme.title: The Chase

programme.title: Genius Game
programme.desc: The eliminated players return to pledge their allegiance, giving their chosen player an advantage in the final game. The finalists face a best-of-three Death Match format that tests the most strategic and lateral thinkers and the first player to win two games will be the winner

programme.title: Genius Game
programme.desc: With only five players remaining, there is everything to play for in the Main Match Media Moguls, a game where only the most astute investors will be rewarded. The winner of the Main Match will automatically secure a place in the final while the loser faces an immediate elimination - as all players vie for the top spot, not everyone is honest about their strategy. David Tennant hosts
